steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what causes meanders to form. The notes state: "A meander forms when water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits that sediment on the opposite side." The correct answer must identify this mechanism.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: identifies the cause — erosion and redeposition of sediment from one bank to the other — which is exactly what the notes say creates meanders. ✓
  • Choice B: discusses sinuosity without explaining what causes meanders to form. ✗
  • Choice C: describes meander behavior, not formation. ✗
  • Choice D: addresses meander existence but not the mechanism that creates them. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
